tasks_completed.description: Updated policies/tool-policy.v1.json: added */docs/codex/ to 11 roles, incremented policy_version to 4, updated _note
tasks_completed.changes: Added '*/docs/codex/' to allowed_write_path_prefixes for ba, qa, pm, architect, product-owner, user, ui-specialist, spec, prompt-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, style-inspector. Incremented policy_version from 3 to 4. Updated _note with 20260521-090100 amendment line. Dev role unchanged (wildcard * already covers docs/codex/).
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: docs/codex/ prefix missing from all 11 consultation roles. Without this, the policy checker blocks writes to docs/codex/ paths even when the tee target is hook-resolvable. N/A commit — missing feature.

tasks_completed.description: Updated commands/codex.md Steps 2-5: canonical path logic with path-safety guard and fallback
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced Step 2 flat /var/tmp path with 2-branch logic: (1) canonical $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt when env vars are non-empty, match ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$, and are not '.' or '..'; (2) fallback /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-$$-... otherwise. Restructured Steps 3/4 to branch on _CODEX_USE_CANONICAL — each tee target is inline hook-resolvable form. Updated Step 5 to remove $CODEX_OUT reference.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: bash_write_targets.py::_resolve_path() cannot expand $CODEX_OUT; hook sees literal token $CODEX_OUT which matches no prefix. Fix uses $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR prefix which _resolve_path() expands correctly.

tasks_completed.description: Updated /root/.agents/skills/codex/SKILL.md: identical Step 2-5 changes as commands/codex.md
tasks_completed.changes: Identical Step 2 path-resolution block and Steps 3/4 branch restructuring applied. Source: cross-link at line 8 preserved. SKILL.md is outside the dot-claude git repo (at /root/.agents/skills/codex/) so appears in untracked state from repo perspective.
tasks_completed.rationale: SKILL.md is the actual runtime Skill invoked by Skill(skill='codex'); changes must be mirrored exactly to eliminate hook-blocked tee invocations from all callers.

tasks_completed.description: Implemented test skeletons for AC1-AC6 (replaced TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels with real assertions)
tasks_completed.rationale: Test skeletons had TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels that hard-fail. Implemented real assertions covering: AC1 Python set comparison of role set, AC2 policy_version integer, AC3 path-safety guard + dotdot rejection + canonical shape + mkdir-p + fallback, AC4 all-tee-sites hook-resolvable both files, AC5 SKILL.md bash block matches codex.md bash block, AC6 /var/tmp only in else branch. All 6 pass.

tasks_completed.description: Add */docs/research/ write-path prefix to ba role in tool-policy.v1.json (M1)
tasks_completed.changes: Added */docs/research/ to ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes; confirmed present at line 139
tasks_completed.rationale: PRIMARY blocker: pretool-tool-policy.py fires for all tools (matcher *) and checks is_allowed('ba','Write','/root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3') which returned (False,'not in allowed_write_path_prefixes'). Adding */docs/research/ unblocks ba subagent redirect targets in that directory.

tasks_completed.description: Extend sentinel block to IS_SUBAGENT=1 in pretool-bash-safety.sh (M2)
tasks_completed.changes: Removed IS_SUBAGENT != 1 guard at former line 483; IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 145 (legacy allowlist firewall) preserved unchanged
tasks_completed.rationale: SECONDARY blocker: sentinel check was entirely skipped for IS_SUBAGENT=1. Only user-created structured sentinel grants (written by userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) are extended to subagent contexts.

tasks_completed.description: Align task ID reader fallback to session_id in pretool-bash-safety.sh (M3)
tasks_completed.changes: Reader now falls back to d.get('session_id','') directly (same source as writer's SID) rather than preferring d.get('task_id','') first
tasks_completed.rationale: Mismatch scenario: CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset, PreToolUse JSON task_id field differs from session_id. Writer uses CLAUDE_TASK_ID:-SID (session_id). Reader now uses same session_id as final fallback.

tasks_completed.description: Skip sentinel write for is_regex=true grants in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (S1)
tasks_completed.changes: Added early exit (exit 0) at Step 4 when IS_REGEX=true; regex grants still write legacy pattern-string grant only
tasks_completed.rationale: CF-1 fix: regex sentinel writes op:'*' which never structurally matches, causing CF-1 to suppress the legacy /allow path. Skipping sentinel write for regex grants preserves legacy path.

tasks_completed.description: Add Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) to settings.json allow list
tasks_completed.changes: Added Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) to allow list at line 69
tasks_completed.rationale: Allows /tmp venv Python interpreters to run without permission prompts for the common data-analysis use case.

tasks_completed.description: Removed .hook-refactor-allow entry from .gitignore so future sentinel files are visible in git status
tasks_completed.changes: Deleted line 77: '.hook-refactor-allow' from the 'Live workflow / dev coordination state' section of .gitignore
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause per context JSON: commit 76f9124 added the sentinel to .gitignore instead of deleting the stale file, making residue invisible in routine git status inspection for 25 days. Removing the gitignore entry ensures any future sentinel file is immediately visible.

tasks_completed.description: Confirmed all 10 documented sentinel files are absent (prior cycle completed deletions)
tasks_completed.changes: No files to delete — all sentinel files confirmed absent at cycle start via os.path.exists() and ls checks
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec tasks 1-3 (delete sentinel files) were completed in a prior cycle. This cycle's role is to complete task 4 (gitignore) and run tests.

tasks_completed.description: Applied Codex-feedback-driven test improvements: live filesystem search + gitignore verification in AC1; removed fragile isdir assertion in AC6
tasks_completed.changes: AC1: Added subprocess find() search over /dev/shm/dev-workspace and /root to detect any new sentinel files; added gitignore content assertion that .hook-refactor-allow line is absent. AC6: Removed os.path.isdir(project_dir) assertion that would fail if a project directory was moved/deleted independently of sentinel cleanup.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex adversarial review identified: (1) no test verified the gitignore change, (2) inventory tests were hardcoded with no live search, (3) AC6 isdir assertion creates false negatives unrelated to sentinel task.

recommendations: Have callers (commands/dev.md, commands/close.md) export CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID and CODEX_OUT_ROLE to subagents automatically (BA spec Should Have S1 — deferred to separate cycle per scope exclusion)

The Codex Skill self-invocation loop should be investigated: when Skill(skill='codex') is used with a complex prompt, the skill's own step-dispatch may consume the prompt as $ARGUMENTS rather than passing it to the codex CLI binary

SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED (Codex finding 1): posttool-allowlist-consume.py exits early for subagents but M2 now lets subagents through the sentinel check. Sentinel remains reusable after a ba subagent Bash call succeeds. Fix: extend sentinel consumption in posttool-allowlist-consume.py to cover subagent Bash events.

SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED (Codex finding 2): posttool-allowlist-consume.py task_id derivation still prefers task_id field before session_id; this is the mirror of the M3 fix. In the mismatch scenario, pretool grants sentinel-ok using session_id key but posttool cannot find and unlink that file. Fix: align posttool task_id fallback to session_id first (same as M3).
These two recommendations are related and should be addressed together in one follow-up cycle targeting posttool-allowlist-consume.py.
Stage and commit .claude/.hook-refactor-allow。 (tracked file, deletion from prior cycle) together with .gitignore in the commit for this cycle

Follow-up cycle: structural hardening — remove the $HOME/.claude/.hook-refactor-allow fallback from pretool-block-production-files.sh:133 to eliminate the permanent global bypass surface (logged as out-of-scope by BA)
